description: A bilingual brand statement declaring core values and mission, emphasizing product benefits aligned with local climate and lifestyle needs.
structural_recipe: Lead with a concise English brand promise (benefit-driven, 1-2 lines). Follow with 3 key pillars: performance claim, primary benefit (skin/health), and lifestyle promise. Mirror the statement in Arabic using equivalent language structure. Use emoji sparingly (sun/climate symbols) to reinforce the core message. Maintain parallel construction between EN and AR versions.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to brands that acknowledge local climate realities and position products as purposefully designed solutions. Bilingual manifestos signal inclusivity and respect for both global and local identity. Clear value statements reduce purchase friction by immediately communicating brand authority and relevance to regional lifestyle needs.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure Arabic translation maintains cultural tone and does not feel like direct word-for-word conversion
Climate-specific claims must be factually accurate and substantiated for skeptical audiences
Balance between English (global positioning) and Arabic (local relevance) should feel intentional, not arbitrary
cultural_constraints: Avoid gendered language unless specifically targeting; use inclusive forms
Climate and environmental references should reflect genuine product adaptation, not greenwashing
Bilingual structure must prioritize Arabic equally to avoid perception of English-first positioning
